shorts.title: The $1,900 Tax Deduction 95% of Freelancers Miss
shorts.description:
Most freelancers overpay by $1,900/year by skipping one simple deduction. Here's how the IRS home office deduction actually works in 2026.

⚠️ Not tax advice. Talk to a CPA.

#taxes #freelancer #taxtips #personalfinance #taxdeduction #moneytips #selfemployed
shorts.thumbnail_prompt: Shocked person looking at tax form, giant red $1900 text overlay, dramatic lighting, cinematic, 9:16 portrait
shorts.voiceover_full: 95 percent of freelancers overpay on taxes. Here's the one deduction they miss. The IRS lets you deduct your home office, but most freelancers skip it because they think it's an audit red flag. It's not. The simplified method is dead simple. Just measure the square footage of your work space, multiply by 5 dollars, and that's your deduction, up to 1500 dollars every year. Add your internet, phone, and a percent of your rent, and freelancers typically find another 400 dollars they were leaving on the table. That's 1900 dollars back in your pocket, legally. I personally claim 1430 every single year. Fifteen minutes of paperwork. Follow for more tax moves they don't teach you.

shorts.title: Your credit score LIES (there are 5 of them)
shorts.description:
Why the credit score you see isn't the one lenders use. 5 FICO versions explained. Save thousands on your next loan.

#creditscore #fico #creditrepair #mortgage #carloan #financialliteracy #moneytips
shorts.thumbnail_prompt: Credit score gauge showing 5 different numbers, confused person silhouette, red warning sign, cinematic 9:16
shorts.voiceover_full: Your credit score isn't three digits. It's actually five different numbers, and lenders use different ones. FICO 8 is the consumer score you see on Credit Karma. But car dealers use FICO Auto Score. Mortgage lenders use FICO 2, 4, and 5. Credit card companies often pull FICO Bankcard. That's why your app says 780 but your car loan offer was 710. Different score, same you. Before any big purchase, pay 30 bucks at my FICO dot com and pull the exact score that lender uses. You'll negotiate better rates. I've seen people save 12 thousand dollars on a mortgage. Follow. Save this for your next loan.

shorts.title: I made $500 in 72h with AI (no face, no inventory)
shorts.description:
How I made $500 selling AI-generated Notion templates on Gumroad. Step-by-step process. No face, no inventory.

#aihustle #sidehustle #passiveincome #notion #gumroad #makemoney #digitalproducts
shorts.thumbnail_prompt: Laptop screen showing $467 bank deposit, clock showing 72h, Claude AI logo, dollar bills floating, 9:16 portrait
shorts.voiceover_full: Last weekend I made 500 dollars selling AI-generated digital products. Here's exactly how. Step one, I used Claude AI to write a Notion productivity template. Took me 45 minutes. Step two, I uploaded it to Gumroad for 9 dollars and 90 cents. Zero inventory. Zero shipping. Step three, I posted a 30-second tutorial on TikTok and Threads. No face, just screen recording. In 72 hours, 51 sales. After Gumroad fees, 467 dollars in my bank. The template still sells every single day. It's called passive income because I literally did the work once. Follow for the exact prompt I used.

shorts.title: The 3-Bucket Rule beats 90% of financial advisors
shorts.description:
A simple 3-bucket investing system that beats 90% of paid advisors. No fees, no complicated math.

#investing #personalfinance #wealthbuilding #indexfunds #investingforbeginners #financialfreedom
shorts.thumbnail_prompt: Three glass buckets labeled Safety Growth Speculation, coins flowing into them, dramatic lighting, 9:16
shorts.voiceover_full: This simple 3-bucket system beats 90 percent of financial advisors. No fees. No complicated math. Bucket one, Safety. Three to six months of expenses in a high-yield savings account. Current best rates, 4.4 percent at places like Marcus or SoFi. Bucket two, Growth. 80 percent of your investing money in a total market index fund. V T I or V O O. Average return historically, 10 percent a year. Bucket three, Speculation. Max 10 percent in individual stocks, crypto, or whatever gets you excited. Lose it all? You still have 90 percent. No advisor. No 1 percent fee eating 300 thousand dollars over your lifetime. Just three accounts and patience. Follow for the setup checklist.

shorts.title: 5 Tax Deductions Freelancers Keep Missing (Costs Them $4K+/yr)
shorts.description:
Five tax deductions every freelancer forgets. Adds up to $4,000+ per year. Health insurance, mileage, education, software, SEP IRA.

#taxes #freelancer #taxtips #sepira #selfemployed #taxdeduction #personalfinance
shorts.thumbnail_prompt: Freelancer at desk with 5 receipt stacks, $4,000 cashback text, calculator, shocked reaction, 9:16
shorts.voiceover_full: Five tax deductions every freelancer forgets, and they cost you thousands every year. Number one, health insurance premiums. If you buy it yourself and you're profitable, deduct 100 percent. Average freelancer, 6 thousand dollars. Number two, business mileage. 67 cents per mile in 2026. Driving to coffee shops, client meetings, even the post office counts. Track it with the MileIQ app. Number three, continuing education. Online courses, books, certifications related to your work. A 500 dollar course costs you 360 after the deduction. Number four, subscription software. Adobe, Notion, Canva, ChatGPT Plus. Every monthly bill adds up. Typical freelancer, 800 dollars a year. Number five, retirement. A SEP IRA lets you hide up to 66 thousand dollars from taxes. Zero financial advisors tell you this. Follow, I'm doing a deep dive on the SEP next.

shorts.title: 0 to 750 credit score in 6 months — exact playbook
shorts.description:
Exact 6-month playbook to build a 750+ credit score from scratch. Secured card → utilization trick → limit increase → second card.

#creditscore #creditbuilding #fico #discover #credithacks #personalfinance #moneytips
shorts.thumbnail_prompt: Credit score meter going from 0 to 750 with big green arrow, 6-month calendar, credit cards fanning out, 9:16
shorts.voiceover_full: Here's how to hit a 750 credit score in 6 months, works even if you're starting from zero. Month one, open a secured credit card. Discover secured has no annual fee. Deposit 300 bucks, that's your limit. Months two and three, use 10 percent of the limit every month, pay it off completely before the statement closes. Not the due date, the statement date. That's the trick. Month four, request a credit limit increase. Same card. They'll approve you if you've been clean. Months five and six, add one more card. Keep total utilization under 10 percent. Your score jumps 80 to 120 points in this window. Don't close old cards. Don't apply for 5 cards at once. Patience. Follow for the exact card list.

shorts.title: 3 Side Hustles That Don't Work Anymore (2026)
shorts.description:
3 side hustles influencers still push that don't make money in 2026. Print-on-demand, dropshipping, crypto trading.

#sidehustle #makemoneyonline #entrepreneur #dropshipping #printondemand #cryptotrading #moneytips
shorts.thumbnail_prompt: Three red X marks over t-shirt, delivery box, and crypto chart, caption DON'T WORK, 9:16
shorts.voiceover_full: Three side hustles influencers keep pushing, that don't actually make money in 2026. One, print-on-demand t-shirts. Market is saturated, ad costs tripled, margins are under 2 dollars per shirt. You need 500 sales a month to matter. Two, dropshipping from AliExpress. Shipping takes 3 weeks, returns crush you, Meta ad costs are insane. Less than 2 percent of new stores survive 6 months. Three, crypto trading. 85 percent of retail traders lose money. The ones selling you courses make money, from you. What actually works, high-margin digital products, AI-automated services, and paid newsletters. Follow, I break each down next.

shorts.title: VTI vs VOO — the REAL answer (I've held both)
shorts.description:
VTI vs VOO compared. Returns, fees, diversification. The real answer most people miss.

#investing #vti #voo #indexfunds #sp500 #stockmarket #personalfinance
shorts.thumbnail_prompt: VTI vs VOO versus boxing ring, stock charts in background, trophy in middle, 9:16
shorts.voiceover_full: V T I or V O O, which one makes you richer? Most people pick wrong. V O O holds the S and P 500, the top 500 US companies. Historical return, 10 percent a year. Fee, 0.03 percent. V T I holds the entire US stock market, around 4000 companies. Includes small and mid caps that grow faster. Historical return, 10.1 percent. Fee, 0.03 percent. Over 30 years, V T I has beaten V O O by a slight margin, but it's almost identical. Both will make you rich if you just keep buying. The real mistake, picking one and then switching every year chasing returns. That costs you 2 percent a year in taxes and fees. Pick one. Stick with it. Follow.

shorts.title: The 6-month emergency fund myth (costs you $180K)
shorts.description:
Why '6 months of expenses' is bad advice for most people. The real rule based on your job stability.

#emergencyfund #personalfinance #investing #savings #financialplanning #moneytips #financialliteracy
shorts.thumbnail_prompt: Piggy bank with 6 months written, red X over it, arrow pointing to stock chart rising, $180K text, 9:16
shorts.voiceover_full: Everyone says save 6 months of expenses. For most people, that's actually bad advice. Six months is for self-employed people with unpredictable income. If you have a stable W-2 job, it's overkill, and you're losing money. Six months of expenses for the average American is around 15 thousand dollars sitting at 4 percent. Meanwhile the S and P returns 10 percent historically. If you have good job security, keep 3 months. If you have a partner also working, 2 months. Put the rest in index funds. Over 30 years, the difference between 6 months cash and 3 months is about 180 thousand dollars you could've had. Follow for more rules they got wrong.

shorts.title: The Roth IRA hack: use it as emergency fund AND retirement
shorts.description:
Your Roth IRA doubles as an emergency fund. Pull your contributions anytime, no penalty. The $1.2M retirement strategy nobody tells you.

#rothira #investing #retirement #personalfinance #fire #wealthbuilding #millionaire
shorts.thumbnail_prompt: Piggy bank with IRA label and million dollar sign, secret unlock key, cinematic gold lighting, 9:16
shorts.voiceover_full: Your Roth IRA is worth 500 thousand dollars more than you think, if you use this one trick. A Roth IRA lets you invest 7 thousand a year, and everything you make inside is tax free forever. Here's what nobody tells you, you can pull out your contributions any time with no penalty. Just not the earnings. That means your Roth doubles as an emergency fund. Max it every year. If you ever need cash, pull your contributions. Earnings keep compounding untouched. Even better, at 59 and a half, everything comes out tax free. Average millennial maxing a Roth from 25 ends with 1.2 million. Zero tax. Follow, I'll show you the auto-invest setup next.

mapping_notes.to_revid: 각 `shorts[n]`이 Revid 영상 1개. Revid의 `/api/public/v2/render` 호출 시 `script` = voiceover_full, `sceneList` = scenes, `voiceId` = brand.tts.voice_id, `captionPresetName` = brand.caption_style, `webhookUrl`로 완료 알림 받기.
mapping_notes.to_typeframes: 동일 API (typeframes = revid 서버). 필드명 같음.
mapping_notes.to_pictory: Pictory API는 `content.videoScript` + `audio.voice` 로 매핑. scenes는 자동 분할.
mapping_notes.to_invideo: InVideo API는 `script` 단일 문자열 + `style_id`. scenes 수동 지정 불필요 (AI가 자동).
mapping_notes.to_freebeat: Freebeat는 Suno 링크 전용. voiceover 있으면 X. 음악만 있는 영상에 사용.
mapping_notes.to_lyricedits: LyricEdits는 lyrics-only. voiceover JSON은 안 맞음. 노래 가사 있으면 그쪽 사용.

production_checklist: 1. ElevenLabs에서 각 voiceover_full 텍스트를 Rachel voice로 TTS 생성 (영상당 $0.30)
2. Revid.ai 가입 (Growth $39/월) → API key 발급 → 'Get API Code'로 실제 스키마 확인
3. 이 JSON의 각 shorts를 Revid POST 요청으로 변환 (mapping_notes 참조)
4. webhookUrl 설정해서 렌더링 완료 시 자동 다운로드
5. 썸네일: Midjourney 또는 FAL AI로 thumbnail_prompt 사용
6. YouTube 업로드: youtube_uploader.py 재사용 가능 (이미 OAuth 있음)
7. 업로드 후 product_promo.py에 영상 URL 추가 → 텔레그램 자동 홍보
